entries.intro: My grandmother would have called it an omen, the way the birds scattered this morning. I think they just got spooked.
details: A whole flock took off at once: no warning, no hawk overhead. Just gone.
She used to say birds know things before people do. I loved her, but she said a lot of things.
reactions: I'm not superstitious. But I did walk home a little faster than usual.

statCheck.pass: Ground vibrations can startle a flock like that: a shifting foundation, maybe a sinkhole forming. Nothing mystical about it.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea what set them off. Probably nothing.

entries.intro: Someone found a dead crow on the path and now half the street thinks it means something.
details: It's a dead bird. Birds die. But try telling that to a woman who grew up on old wives' tales.
She moved it off the path with a stick and wouldn't touch it with her hands. Then she burned sage at her door.
reactions: People believe what they believe. No harm in it, I suppose: unless they start making decisions based on a dead crow.

entries.intro: There's an old saying that when the fog sits low around here, the season's about to turn. Seems to hold true more often than not.
details: I've watched for it the last few years and it's been right three times out of four.
My father tracked it too. He kept a little tally on the doorframe: marks for right, crosses for wrong.
reactions: I don't think it's magic: just weather doing what weather does. But it's a handy thing to know.

statCheck.pass: Low fog usually means the ground temperature's shifting faster than the air. That does track with seasonal change: it's just reading the weather, not the future.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why it works. Maybe it doesn't and I'm just remembering the hits.

entries.intro: An old-timer down the lane says the dogs have been restless at night. He thinks it's a bad sign.
details: His hound wouldn't settle down all week: pacing, whining at the door. The neighbors' dogs were the same.
He says animals sense things people can't. His wife says they sense raccoons and nothing more.
reactions: I'm with the wife on this one. But I did check my shutters before bed last night.

statCheck.pass: Dogs pick up on low sounds and ground tremors that we miss. Doesn't mean anything bad: could be burrowing animals or shifting soil.
statCheck.fail: I don't know enough about dogs to say. Mine sleeps through everything.

entries.intro: The moon had a ring around it the other night. Beautiful, actually, but people got nervous.
details: An old woman on my street said it means storms within three days. We'll see.
My uncle used to call it 'the moon wearing a hat.' Said it's nothing: just ice up high catching the light.
reactions: I thought it looked nice. Not everything pretty has to mean something terrible.
